action.hypothesis: Round 75 closed the simplest single-stage `topk` alternative: under the matched `K=128`, `EXPANSION_FACTOR=12`, `signum`, `LR=2.0e-3` recipe it was clearly worse than the `two_stage_residual` mainline, so the next and only remaining high-value matched architecture probe this round should target a different deployment tradeoff rather than another plain top-k variant. `jumprelu` is the best single yes/no probe because it preserves LUTurbo's static-library finite weighted-sum form while potentially reducing online selection complexity through thresholded support shaping instead of a full staged residual path. This round asks one question only: under the exact current mainline recipe, is `jumprelu` competitive enough in full-curve behavior to remain worth later consideration, or should it be ruled out as clearly inferior?
action.summary: Use the one allowed matched architecture probe on `jumprelu`. Keep the current mainline recipe fixed at `K=128`, `EXPANSION_FACTOR=12`, `OPTIMIZER=signum`, `LR=2.0e-3`, and the same aux/runtime settings, and change only `ARCHITECTURE` from `two_stage_residual` to `jumprelu`. Judge the run by the whole trajectory, not just the endpoint: if `jumprelu` stays reasonably close with a healthy curve, keep it in reserve because its thresholded single-stage support path may lower LUTurbo online selection cost; if it is clearly worse throughout, close the probe and return to the `two_stage_residual` mainline next round.
action.self_review: This obeys the Round 76 rule by changing exactly one primary variable, `architecture`, while keeping `K`, `EXPANSION_FACTOR`, `OPTIMIZER`, `LR`, and the rest of the recipe fixed. `jumprelu` is a better use of the single probe than another standard top-k family because it tests a distinct support-selection mechanism that may be cheaper to deploy online while still satisfying the export contract. The main risk is that prior matched probing under an earlier regime already suggested weak reconstruction quality, but this is still the most informative remaining architecture-worthiness check under the current mainline anchor.
notes_to_memory: Round 76 spends the single allowed matched architecture probe on `jumprelu` against the current `two_stage_residual` mainline recipe, because it represents a distinct thresholded selector that may reduce LUTurbo online selection cost while preserving static exportability.

Judge this probe as a family-worthiness test only: compare matched `jumprelu@128` against the `two_stage_residual` reference at fixed `EF=12`, `signum`, and `LR=2.0e-3`, then return to the `two_stage_residual` mainline next round regardless of outcome.
next_hypotheses: If matched `jumprelu` is reasonably close with a healthy curve, return to the `two_stage_residual` mainline next round but keep one later `jumprelu` revisit available because its selector path may be cheaper to deploy.
If matched `jumprelu` is clearly worse than `two_stage_residual` across the curve, close the probe and return to the `two_stage_residual` mainline next round.

If matched `jumprelu` unexpectedly beats the current `two_stage_residual` reference, still return to the `two_stage_residual` mainline next round per policy, but record `jumprelu` as a high-priority later follow-up family.
